rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings with existing features or declared variables.
Generate boolean range expressions using declared helper variables with matching types.
Avoid referencing AADL properties or unit tokens directly in AGREE expressions.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid within annex blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Component type contracts should not use subcomponent ports unless represented through valid variables.
Implementation contracts must bind to actual output features or auxiliary variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Boolean range checks should use helper declarations with matching types.
Assign statements must reference existing output features or declared variables.
Guarantee statements are not allowed in implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL property names as AGREE variables.
Do not use Boolean domain values in numeric comparisons.
Do not generate clauses without visible feature or constant references.
